channel.title: Convert Kong Gateway Services into MCP Tools :: API Management with Kong Konnect
channel.description: In order to implement this use case we are going to expose a sample application implementing a Marketplace. Here are the steps to implement the use case: Use the Kong Mocking plugin to mock an OpenAPI spec (OAS) as Kong Gateway Services and Routes. Apply AI MCP Proxy plugin to convert and expose the Kong Services as MCP Tools. You can now click Next to proceed further.

item.title: Mock Kong Gateway Services with OpenAPI Spec
item.description: The Mocking plugin allows you to provide mock endpoints to test APIs in development against your existing services. The Mocking plugin leverages standards based on the Open API Specification (OAS) for sending out mock responses to APIs. Mocking supports both Swagger 2.0 and OpenAPI 3.0. In this first step we are just creating a Kong Gateway Service and a Kong Route with the Kong Mocking Plugin and the OpenAPI Spec. The next step will convert the Kong Gateway Service into MCP Tools.

item.title: Convert Kong Gateway Services into MCP Tools
item.description: Now, we are going to use the AI MCP Proxy plugin to convert the Kong Gateway Service into MCP Tools. Here’s the new architecture: Download the new decK file Download the marketplace_mcp.yaml Kong decK spec file. Submit the decK declaration to your Control Plane deck gateway reset --konnect-control-plane-name kong-workshop --konnect-token $PAT -f deck gateway sync --konnect-control-plane-name kong-workshop --konnect-token $PAT marketplace_mcp.yaml You should see two Kong Gateway Services now:

item.title: Test the MCP Tools and LLM with a Strands Agent
item.description: Since we have all components in place, we can extend our Strands Agent to take advantage of the MCP Tools Here’s the new code: cat > kong-workshop-agent.py << 'EOF' from strands import Agent from strands.models.openai import OpenAIModel from strands.tools.mcp import MCPClient from mcp.client.streamable_http import streamablehttp_client import os data_plane_lb = os.getenv("DATA_PLANE_LB"); kong_dp = f"http://{data_plane_lb}" kong_dp_route = f"http://{data_plane_lb}/anthropic-route" print("kong_dp_route") print(kong_dp_route) openai_model = OpenAIModel( client_args={ "base_url": kong_dp_route, "api_key": "dummy", }, model_id="claude-sonnet-4-6", ) streamable_http_mcp_listener_client = MCPClient(lambda: streamablehttp_client(f"{kong_dp}/mcp-listener")) with streamable_http_mcp_listener_client: tools = streamable_http_mcp_listener_client.list_tools_sync() for tool in tools: print(tool.tool_spec) agent = Agent(model=openai_model, tools=tools) agent("List all orders made by Alice Johnson") EOF The main update here is the inclusion the of Serviceless Route we created in the previous step which defines the “/mcp-listener” path.
